name: DiVincenzo Criteria
summary: A classic checklist for what a physical system must provide to support quantum computation: scalable qubits, initialization, long coherence, universal gates, and measurement.
whenToUseIt: Use it when comparing hardware platforms or asking whether a quantum technology is merely interesting physics or a plausible computing architecture.
steps: Check whether the platform offers a scalable qubit representation.
Ask how states are initialized and reset.
Assess coherence time relative to control operations.
Confirm that a universal gate set can be implemented.
Evaluate whether individual qubits can be measured reliably.
example: A platform might have excellent coherence but poor scaling or weak measurement. The DiVincenzo criteria help explain why that still limits its computing potential.
